title: Your Attitudes to Continuity and One True Canon
display_title: Your Attitudes to Continuity and One True Canon
wikitext:
[]: Your Attitudes to Continuity and "One True Canon"             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 12:49 pm Evangelion has quite a fair few different AUs and sub-continuities (although nowhere near the confusing levels of whuh? of, say, Transformers, The Legend of Zelda, Final Fantasy or the like). Whilst they're not that difficult to differentiate from each other due to how different they are, they still cause a lot of arguments. So, basically the point of this thread is to discuss what you consider to be THE canon, AUs, totally separate (but still good) continuities, alternate endings, things you consider non-canonical but are otherwise totally indifferent to, and things which you consider totally non-canonical and would like to believe never existed(the way that some Star Wars fans refuse to accept the prequels). My personal beliefs: The original series is the "One True Canon", the canon to compare all other Eva works to, the original, the Alpha and the Omega. It CAN be expanded upon, but not rewritten. That's not to say that nothing will ever surpass it, but if, say, an actual remake TV series of it is made, it will be an (admittedly still no doubt brilliant) AU/separate continuity rather than a replacement for the original canon. Episodes 25/26 were the closest thing to the true, canonical ending to the series that exists. Technically, there is no canonical ending-the "canonical" ending is that the ending is totally up to interpretation (my interpretation being that the characters finally learnt to overcome their psychological issues and find some level of happiness). On that note, End of Evangelion, and all scenes therein (including that scene) never happened, and is completely non-canonical-and no, I don't buy the theory that the original ending was an alternate perspective of what happened from Shinji's point of view-as far as I'm concerned, instrumentality, Asuka/Misato's deaths, the hospital scene, and all other such horrific events, did not happen in any way, shape or form. (Note that this isn't for reasons of quality, as it's a fantastic film, but quite frankly it's so depressing and disturbing that I like to forget it ever existed. Also it kinda ruined Shinji's character, and is at least partially responsible for the huge amount of hate the poor kid receives). Rebuild is a completely valid and acceptable canon, but it is separate from TOS, and thus they are both equally canonical and excellent in their own respective (but different) universes. I have not read the manga, but as with Rebuild I treat it as a totally valid but completely separate continuity. The PS2/PSP game is itself simply a non-canonical AU. The tidbits and classified information (such as the origins of Adam and Lilith), however, is canonical, as it gives proper explanations to things from the original series without invalidating anything already established. I know very little about the other games, but to me they're non-canonical. The Shinji Ikari Raising Project and Girlfriend of Steel mangas, once again, I haven't read, but from what I've seen of them I treat them as little more than non-canonical AUs. After the End is hard to classify, as it sits outside the canon meter due to completely and utterly breaking the 4th wall. I don't consider it canonical (it is, after all, extremely silly) but I love it so much that I consider it the "canonical alternate ending" if that makes sense. Anyway, what are YOUR opinions?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 17, 2013 12:59 pm Every work is self contained. The original series is a distinct continuity from 1-24 + EoE. No continuity supersedes any of the others, yet no continuity falsifies any of the others. They're all equally unreal.

[]: Re: Your Attitudes to Continuity and "The One True Cano             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:03 pm Squigsquasher wrote: (Note that this isn't for reasons of quality, as it's a fantastic film, but quite frankly it's so depressing and disturbing that I like to forget it ever existed.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:17 pm Evangelion, consisting of, what else, Neon Genesis Evangelion, Death, and End of Evangelion is the true masterpiece. I can't see how anything more need be said. The other "versions" are of course equally fictional, but always inferior.

[]: Re: Your Attitudes to Continuity and "One True Canon&qu             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:21 pm Squigsquasher wrote: On that note, End of Evangelion, and all scenes therein (including that scene) never happened, and is completely non-canonical-and no, I don't buy the theory that the original ending was an alternate perspective of what happened from Shinji's point of view-as far as I'm concerned, instrumentality, Asuka/Misato's deaths, the hospital scene, and all other such horrific events, did not happen in any way, shape or form. (Note that this isn't for reasons of quality, as it's a fantastic film, but quite frankly it's so depressing and disturbing that I like to forget it ever existed. Also it kinda ruined Shinji's character, and is at least partially responsible for the huge amount of hate the poor kid receives).

[]: Re: Your Attitudes to Continuity and "One True Canon&am             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:53 pm Ah, Bagheera, my old nemesis, and corrector of all my canon blunders. Yes I am joking. You're awesome dude. Bagheera wrote: You do realize we see Misato and Ritsuko dead in EoTV, right?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 17, 2013 1:53 pm Another concurrency + death of the author argument waiting to happen. Awesome. I also prefer the original broadcast version, and consider the DC episodes + EoE to be it's own separate 'branch' resulting from several small retcons.

[]: Re: Your Attitudes to Continuity and "One True Canon&am             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:09 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Yes, indeed I do. I thought that those were simply little snippits of "what could have been" by Anno and co, showing what they originally had in mind, and thus weren't actually indicative of what was actually going on (Misato gets her own "case" after all).

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:14 pm Damnit, Bagheera, stop being so smart, you're making me feel inadequate! ...Ah well. So much for that. RIP, Misato, Queen of Beer, you will be missed. Still, I can at least take comfort in the knowledge that it's completely up to the viewer whether THAT scene is canonical or not, right? ...Right?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:15 pm Game with multiple endings = superposition. They all happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:20 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Still, I can at least take comfort in the knowledge that it's completely up to the viewer whether THAT scene is canonical or not, right? ...Right?

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:22 pm Bagheera wrote: If you're dead set on broadcast version, sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:27 pm Squigsquasher wrote: *stuff*

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:30 pm No, your ass is getting saved even if you hide under the desk. The folly of man. Furniture cannot stave off the eschaton.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 2:59 pm Lavinius wrote: I don't think you understand End of Evangelion, not even the littlest bit. Death means nothing before Instrumentality. It's a Divine Comedy: anyone who wants to be saved can be.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 17, 2013 5:23 pm Oh boy, the old faithful concurrency debate with a side of "Is EoE a hopeful ending?" rolled into the mix. I take the different endings as akin to different game endings -- EoE is just one of the ones you can get in the NGE2 game, after all; whereas EoTV is something like a Gendo True End, even if not actually included as such in the game. The style of the endings reflect Anno's mindset at the time (in remission at the end of the series, depressed again when making the movie). I am also wryly amused by his volte face on the subject of post-apocalyptic worlds, where he pretty much reaches the conclusion that EoE would be a BAD END and not a clean slate fresh start.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 17, 2013 5:41 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Oh, I understood it all right (Indeed, any of our beloved characters COULD come back, save perhaps Yui, who is drifting through space forever, Gendo, whose soul was destroyed utterly by Yui, and possibly Rei and Kaworu, who I think finally allowed themselves to die during Instrumentality's rejection)...but the damage cannot be undone. Shinji and Asuka cannot take back the abuse that they dealt each other. Ritsuko will still have experienced being shot in the head by a man she once loved. Maya will still have watched Asuka being devoured alive. Asuka, for her part, still remembers being devoured alive. Misato was still fatally wounded and blown in half. And then there's all the JSSDF soldiers and NERV employees that were either massacred or tanged. Even though there is the possibility for everybody to come back, the things still happened to them.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jul 17, 2013 5:44 pm Only because Misato will no doubt spend her newfound life trying to convert LCL into alcohol. Nothing gets between that woman and a drink, not even death itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Thu Jul 18, 2013 4:07 pm everything is canon and everything is non-canon it all happened and it all didn't happen

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Jul 18, 2013 4:34 pm I mash TV and EoE somehow in my brain together. I don't even think too much about it, so both are valid in their own way.

Whilst they're not that difficult to differentiate from each other due to how different they are, they still
